channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Lone star ticks are covering much of the U.S. Here's what you need to know
item.description: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.
item.content:encoded: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: 5 safety tips to keep you out of the emergency room this summer
item.description: Experts share guidance on how to prevent common summertime health risks, such as drowning, fire accidents and heat exhaustion.
item.content:encoded: Experts share guidance on how to prevent common summertime health risks, such as drowning, fire accidents and heat exhaustion.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Kids with autism are prone to drowning. Florida is trying to prevent that
item.description: Kids with autism can be 160 times more likely than other children to drown. Florida is dedicating state money to a program that prioritizes swim lessons for these kids.
item.content:encoded:
Kids with autism can be 160 times more likely than other children to drown. Florida is dedicating state money to a program that prioritizes swim lessons for these kids.

(Image credit: LA Johnson)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
item.description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
item.content:encoded: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey

item.title: FDA scientists flag concerns with peptides, the trendy molecules RFK Jr. supports
item.description: Documents reviewing the research on peptides note lack of good evidence of efficacy or safety. Yet an FDA panel will consider easing access to them later this month.
item.content:encoded:
Documents reviewing the research on peptides note lack of good evidence of efficacy or safety. Yet an FDA panel will consider easing access to them later this month.

(Image credit: Svitlana Pietukhova/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: After Trump's reelection, these U.S. scientists found jobs in the U.K.
item.description: More U.S. scientists are heading abroad. Three researchers explain why they decided to shift their research to universities in the U.K.
item.content:encoded: More U.S. scientists are heading abroad. Three researchers explain why they decided to shift their research to universities in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Sing-along time at a memory cafe is a highlight for people dealing with dementia
item.description: Music tends to stay with people even as dementia claims other memories. In Vermont, Memory Cafes provide communities for both dementia patients and their caregivers.
item.content:encoded: Music tends to stay with people even as dementia claims other memories. In Vermont, Memory Cafes provide communities for both dementia patients and their caregivers.
item.dc:creator: Nina Keck

item.title: They were world-class tennis rivals. Now friends, they've teamed up against cancer
item.description: They were the women's tennis champions of their generation. Now, Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova open up about friendship, cancer and retirement in the documentary Chris & Martina: The Final Set.
item.content:encoded:
They were the women's tennis champions of their generation. Now, Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova open up about friendship, cancer and retirement in the documentary Chris & Martina: The Final Set.

(Image credit: Trevor Jones)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: People are taking allergy and heartburn pills for PMS. Could it work?
item.description: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.
item.content:encoded: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden

item.title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
item.description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
item.content:encoded:
Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.

(Image credit: Allison Lee Isley)
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey
